anecdotes.story: I went surfing for the only time ever and I borrowed a board from a neighbor. After paddling around a while I finally decided to go for it and I caught a wave. As I was riding the wave in I found that I was heading straight for a big coral outcropping. I thought I was going to destroy the guy's board, so I slipped into the water and tried to steer the board away from the coral. It's hard to steer a surfboard once it has decided to go. I hit the coral feet first. It was a bit painful, since coral is pretty sharp, and this particular coral was the home of a lot of sea urchins. I protected the surfboard and managed to get back to the beach where I started cleaning up several cuts and scrapes and carefully removing sea urchin spines. Personally I didn't find surfing to be all that fun.
anecdotes.question: As a teenager living right by the beach I spent a lot of time in the ocean. So, what was my experience with surfing?
answers: I became the junior surfing champion of Puerto Rico.
I loved surfing and did it all the time.

anecdotes.story: My football coach drove one. He was mean. I have no idea why I continued with football, except that I was taught not to be a quitter. I really didn't like that man and dreaded the thought of seeing him. So, whenever I saw a car that looked like his I'd go out of my way to avoid walking anywhere near it.
anecdotes.question: Why did I walk on the other side of the street whenever I saw a blue Volkswagen?
answers: I thought it was a mafia car.
It was always parked near the crazy coconut guy.

anecdotes.story: I went to Guyana with my Dad when I was about 13. We went to the zoo and there was a monkey tied to a pole with a little house on top. There was a border of rocks in a circle around the pole to keep people from getting within reach of the monkey. I got up close to the rocks to see the little guy. He came out slowly to me and we looked at each other for a minute. I learned that he could reach beyond the rocks when he grabbed my glasses and raced back up the pole to his little house. A kind soul went in and got my glasses back for me.
anecdotes.question: I got some unwanted attention at the zoo when -
answers: A horse stepped on my foot.
My foot got caught in the train tracks.
I got caught feeding the gorillas.

anecdotes.story: When I was about 8 years old we got a German shepherd puppy named Franz. As he got bigger he was a little too protective of my Mom and my sister, and wouldn’t let anyone near them. He kind of went crazy every time the garbage man or milk man came to the house. A nice lady who lived on a big farm offered to take him since she had a lot of room. Later he became a daddy and was very protective of his little girl puppy – but that’s another story.
anecdotes.question: Why did we have our first dog for only two years?
answers: He got killed fighting a bear to protect my sister.
He was hit by a truck trying to protect my sister.
He was sent away to a farm because he went crazy.

anecdotes.story: We were in Trinidad at a park near a river. It was a nice big grassy park with some bushes near the edge of the water. I saw something moving near the water and went to investigate. I was quite surprised to see a sea cow, a manatee, munching grass from the river bank. I pulled up some grass and held it out to him and he ate it from my hand. It was about like feeding a cow, except that he was in the water.
anecdotes.question: One day at a picnic with my family I saw a critter watching me through some bushes nearby and I went over and fed him some grass. What kind of critter was it?

anecdotes.story: My big brother Mike was up to bat playing baseball in our driveway. I toddled out to stand by him just as he swung the bat. He didn’t see me. Apparently it was pretty messy because there was a huge bloody mess on my face where my left eye used to be. Of course I don’t remember it because I was only three at the time, but I was in the hospital for a while. The doctors did a good job putting me back together and I didn’t die. I still have a scar above my left eye below the eyebrow, but it’s kind of hard to see now.
anecdotes.question: My first near-death experience was when I was three years old.
answers: My big brother hit me in the head with a baseball bat
I was almost hit by a car while riding my tricycle across the street
I was attacked by a big dog

anecdotes.story: During my spy training at the Farm we were supposed to set up lights around a field so a small plane could land at night to help our agent escape from the country. Unfortunately, during this particular exercise, our mission was going to fail because the terrorists knew of our plans and they were going to capture us. However, the day before the exercise we got a phone call that said I had to go home because my wife was going to have a baby. And that’s how Monica saved me from being captured by terrorists. I’m so thankful.
anecdotes.question: How did Monica save me from being captured by terrorists?
answers: She cried so loudly that they ran away.
She was born at the right time.

anecdotes.story: I didn’t actually work on the yearbook, although I designed one for my Journalism class.  I did all the other stuff.  The Honor Society members were invited to write speeches for graduation and present them before a panel of teachers.  My speech was selected so I spoke at my HS graduation.  I worked in the student store as part of being a Teacher’s Assistant.  I made popcorn in the student store and sold it during the breaks before and after class. And, yes, I was the newspaper editor.  At one time I wanted to be a famous journalist. Good thing I didn’t become one because Dad wouldn’t like me then.
anecdotes.question: In high school I was not:

anecdotes.story: I received a lovely little doll wearing a beautiful wedding dress, and as I recall her arms and legs could bend and her eyes could open and close. I think I also received the brown dump truck that year because I gave Wedding Doll rides around the house in the back of the dump truck. It was a metal truck – like a Tonka truck.
anecdotes.question: One of my favorite Christmas presents when I was 4 was:

anecdotes.story: My Grandpa May had a bike shop in Baker, Oregon.  He gave each grandchild a bike when they turned 8, so when I had my 8th birthday I received a pink Schwinn bicycle from Grandpa.  While learning to ride that bike I crashed into a 2 foot high cinderblock wall, somehow scraping my neck underneath my chin. Ugh.  I had to go to school with bandages and bruises all over my neck.  So, what?  You don't think that is near death???
anecdotes.question: What was one of my earliest near death experiences?

anecdotes.story: I wasn’t very good, but I always attended the tennis club matches so I received a gold and black letter J. (Jackson Jr. High) My dad bought me a tennis racket and arranged for private lessons but I never was very good.  The last time I played tennis was in Japan… and that wasn’t really playing—more like playing around.  Remember Barnaby chasing the cicadas? Ick.
anecdotes.question: In Junior High I received a school letter for what?

anecdotes.story: Our bedroom had hot pink carpet and bedspreads, beads in the doorway, a black light with blacklight posters along with the Butch Cassidy and Glen Campbell posters, but we did not have a telephone in our room.  We did have a record player so we could play Glen Campbell records till our ears fell off.  Our ears never fell off.
anecdotes.question: Which of the following was not in Gina’s and my bedroom when we were growing up?
answers: poster of Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid

anecdotes.story: My dad gave my mom a dachshund puppy before they had any kids.  They named the dog Chris.  When I was born, Chris was already a part of the family.  So, as a baby I would lie on a blanket on the floor and Chris would be at the edge of my blanket looking at me.  I’m sure my love of dachshunds happened because of her cute long nose face next to mine.  (My face didn’t have a long nose.)
anecdotes.question: The first animal I was attached to was:

anecdotes.story: My dad, Warner Newberger, was an air force pilot in Ardmore, Oklahoma when I was born.  I guess you could say I was an air force brat.  However, shortly after I was born Dad left the Air Force and we moved to Pendleton, Oregon.  He worked at a frozen pea factory for a short time before moving to Albuquerque where he started his career as an air traffic controller.  He worked at a movie theater in Provo when he was going to high school. He worked in the BYU photo lab and was a photographer in Provo during his college years.
anecdotes.question: When I was born, my Dad ...
answers: worked in a frozen pea factory
took tickets at a movie theater

anecdotes.story: Actually, A and B are correct. One day I was out running a bunch of errands, including a stop at the bank.  It wasn’t until I got back in the car that I realized that my windbreaker was INSIDE out!  The label, the pockets, the lining… everything hanging out.  I felt rather silly!  One day at church I discovered I was wearing one black shoe and one blue shoe.  Never choose your shoes in a dark closet.  I would never wear purple pants!
anecdotes.question: One day I showed my true fashion sense by ...
answers: wearing one blue and one black shoe to church
forgetting to take a curler out of my hair before going to early morning seminary
wearing a red shirt with purple pants

anecdotes.story: I love all of those books, and Ender's Game isn't even my favorite of them. But since I don't reread books much anymore, its record of thirteen readings will probably stand for the rest of my life. I read that book over and over again when I first got it. I've even owned *two* signed copies of it! My recommendation for most folks, though: Last of the Breed, by Louis L'amour. Seriously. Just read it.
anecdotes.question: What fiction book have I read the most times?

anecdotes.story: I said at the time that I didn't want to spend my life working with computers. And I really do love advertising! I love slogans and condensing ideas down to their bare essence and finding catchy ways to convey them. I love graphic design and... Ooh, I love fonts!
anecdotes.question: What was my undergraduate degree in?

anecdotes.story: I was only a year older than the oldest campers, when I worked as a summer camp counselor in Frankfurt, Germany at the "HICOG" American housing complex.
anecdotes.question: What was my first job?

anecdotes.story: One day during my job as a summer camp counselor, the kids watched Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles on TV. After, I heard a group of boys talking and one said "hey, I bet I can make Tom cry." I walked over to investigate and the kid kicked me right in the crotch. I cried. TV caused violence.
anecdotes.question: Does TV cause violence?
answers: Well, sometimes, it might trigger it
No, but the radiation from it can cause cancer

anecdotes.story: The owner was selling the business. I'd worked there all summer, and had only a month left. He asked me what I'd done to deserve a raise. In truth I was a terrible baker, and Great Harvest already paid very well for an entry retail job. So I just kept working for the same pay under the new owners until I went back to college. Which was very soon, thankfully. I was a terrible baker.
anecdotes.question: What was the first job I ever asked for a raise at?

anecdotes.story: Also Korean, at BYU after my mission. I feel like I'm terrible at memorizing things, but my strength with languages, I think, is because I can 'understand' them more than just memorizing vocabulary.
anecdotes.question: What languages have I studied?

anecdotes.story: I took trombone lessons for one year. I never learned to read music, though -- I just memorized every song we had to play. I still can't read music, though I love to play piano songs I memorize, too. And I still love the trombone line in "Sleigh Ride."
anecdotes.question: What instrument did I study?

anecdotes.story: Mom bought the whole Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves Soundtrack instead of just the 'single' album I'd asked for, and I knew that cost an extra dollar. Even as I threw myself on the floor crying, I knew in my brain that I was being very foolish. And then Mom told me she was just giving me the CD as a present anyway.
anecdotes.question: What caused the last temper tantrum I had as a kid?

anecdotes.story: I met the official drivers and the diplomatic-plated Embassy car on the front steps of the chancery, and helped the ambassador's wife to the car. We had a nice brunch with the Ambassador's wives from France, England, and Australia. They were all very nice!
anecdotes.question: Why did I get a $200 box of chocolate for free in Kuwait?
answers: For escorting the wife of the US Ambassador to the grand opening of a new mall
For volunteering at a women's museum
For guessing the right number of beads in the jar at an Indian Fabric Festival
For having the fastest time in the Desert Girl Triathlon at the embassy

anecdotes.story: I had just been in Egypt! I was feeling very in-touch with the modern, modest women of Egypt and I wanted to cover my hair, too. I got a lot of funny looks, but Tom still recognized me, particularly when I went right up and kissed him.
anecdotes.question: What did I do to help Tom find me at the airport in London?
answers: Make a sign that said "Welcome Home, Bruce!"
Page him over the intercom to meet me at Customer Service
Wear a hijab to cover my hair
Play loud polka music on my phone

anecdotes.story: We assigned utensils -- like spatulas and soup spoons and measuring cups -- numbers, and then everyone had to draw a number. Whatever you got was what you had to eat Thanksgiving dinner with! If you got the teaspoon, you were still in better luck than those who had to use the potato masher...
anecdotes.question: What unusual tradition did my family have for Thanksgiving when I was a kid?
answers: Sleep in the living room the night before and tell stories about the Ghost of Thanksgiving
Eat dinner with random utensils and tools
Coat the turkey with chocolate and cherries
Eat dinner outside by the pool

anecdotes.story: My home teacher offered to help me learn to drive. The first time he picked me up, he gave me the keys to his car and had us drive straight to the freeway, where I had to merge with crazy California traffic!
anecdotes.question: Who taught me how to drive?

anecdotes.story: I failed Physical Science my Freshman Year, but spent the following Spring studying everyday in the TA office and learning everything in and out. I got an A in the class, and was asked to be a TA, but I already had a job as an RA for my Sophomore year. During the Spring, Summer, and my Junior & Senior Year I worked as a TA in the lab, helping students, as well as teaching my own Homework Labs for an hour each week per lab. My favorite experience was teaching a portion of the final exam review in the huge Joseph Smith Bldg auditorium to 1,000 students at the end of my Junior Year.
anecdotes.question: What was your favorite job while at BYU?

anecdotes.story: My call was to Chile, Santiago North. However, I did not serve this mission, as you may know. I decided to stay home and marry Tom. Together we wrote a letter to the Prophet explaining why I would not be serving. It was a stressful decision to make!
anecdotes.question: Where was your mission call to?

anecdotes.story: While all of the above were actual declared majors of mine at one point or another, Theater Education was the 2nd. I was rebelling against the MFHD major of Freshman Year, which had very boring classes. Theater was a lot of fun, but not for me---I wasn't weird enough. Linguistics, Junior Year, was a bust as well (my syntax teacher was so horrible, I couldn't finish his class), so I settled for Global Studies as a Senior because I like traveling and maps.
anecdotes.question: What did you change your major to in your Sophomore year of College?

anecdotes.story: I was asked to be the Yearbook Photographer two weeks before school started. While initially signed up to be in the Women's Show Choir for the 3rd year in a row, I was still sad and upset I had not made the elite Coed Show Choir. So when this chance for something completely new came up, I jumped at the chance and had the best senior year developing a new talent and hobby.
anecdotes.question: Why did you quit choir your Senior Year of High School?
answers: To focus on Track and Field
I lost my voice and missed the audition
I didn't want to wear the immodest costumes

anecdotes.story: I was fast. I was the 2nd fastest girl in Elementary school and I knew I wanted to be a Runner in the Olympics. I had started watching the Olympics and was in awe over Track and Field events. After I retired from Olympics, I was going to be a P.E. Teacher because I loved my P.E. teacher Ms. Kemp and I wanted to be just like her. At various times in my childhood I also wanted to be an artist or the first female President.
anecdotes.question: When I was 10 year old, what did I want to be when I grew up?

anecdotes.story: The Golden Plates is the best primary song! I've picked it a hundred times. Every time I get to choose the song, that's the one I pick.  if you hang in there I'm sure your patience will be rewarded as more stories are surely forthcoming.
anecdotes.question: What is my favorite Primary song?
answers: Jesus Wants Me for a Sunbeam

anecdotes.story: Mom was taking us to the Indian restaurant in the Palms Hotel and as the man in front of us opened the door, the wind caught his robes and blew them back and caught my attention. I cried out to Mom, "It's Jesus!" He didn't seem to hear, but maybe he was just being polite. But they all dress like Jesus over there.
anecdotes.question: How did I greet the first Arab man I encountered in Kuwait?

anecdotes.story: Pyloric stenosis is when the tube leading out of your tummy is blocked shut, so food just stays stuck in your tummy. I kept vomiting and Mom and Dad took me to the doctor. They did an ultrasound and the doctor said Mom and Dad were overreacting. "It's not projectile vomit, so he's fine." So I vomited all over him, from across the room. Then he said, "oh, maybe that kid does have pyloric stenosis. Let's get him in for surgery!"
anecdotes.question: What almost killed me when I was a baby?

anecdotes.story: Ms. Tanio, my kindergarten teacher, invited me to read the book out loud to my class. I read the first part, but then I got tired. It's a really long book. So she finished reading it.
anecdotes.question: Where did I first read "How the Grinch Stole Christmas?"
answers: On a bus for a field trip

anecdotes.story: I don't remember much about it, but Dad says I watched the movie Cars with him seven times in one day, while we were in the hospital. Mom and Clark went to Disneyland and rode rides and had fun. Dad and I sat in the hospital and I had lots of tests done. I didn't like the hospital very much. Next time, I just want to enjoy the park.
anecdotes.question: What did I do at our hotel when we first arrived at Disneyworld?
answers: Run up to Chip and Dale and salute them
Knock over a flaming tiki torch and set Goofy's tail on fire
Have a seizure, pass out on the floor, and vomit
See an elephant and start trumpeting at him to get his attention

anecdotes.story: We arrived a little late. As soon as we sat down, on the side in the very front where everyone could see us, I leaned forward and threw up all over the floor. Everyone was super-nice, and they wrapped me up in a pashmina and took good care of me.
anecdotes.question: How did I greet members of my new ward on my first Friday in Kuwait?
answers: Wrap myself up in my blanket like a burka and refuse to look at anyone
Ran up to the podium and said 'hello everybody!'

anecdotes.story: She was like a little angel, and I did want to keep her. But she was pooping. I could feel it, right when I held her. It was gross! I don't want to hold a baby who's pooping!
anecdotes.question: What did I say the first time I held my baby sister?
answers: She's like a little angel from heaven.

anecdotes.story: I liked the tower -- Taipei 101! We could see it from the airplane as we landed. It was so tall it stuck up through the clouds.
anecdotes.question: What did I like best about Taipei?
answers: Hiking the hills of Jiu-fen in the rain
Watching TV in our hotel room

anecdotes.story: We had to stand in line to get the poop hats, and I didn't really want to wear it, but we all had to have one before we could go down the giant toilet slide into the sewer. We climbed up the side of a giant toilet and then as we slid down, it made a flushing sound. Then we walked out through a tunnel that was lit up with sewer pictures.
anecdotes.question: What was my favorite part of the Poop Museum in Yokohama?
answers: Unchi-man, the superhero of well-formed poop
Sliding down the toilet with my poop hat into the sewer

anecdotes.story: I only liked Japanese baby food. Mom fed me the kind that came with the formula bottles and couldn't read the labels, so she didn't know what flavor it was until a Japanese friend told her, "oh, you're feeding her liver and fish paste? That's great."
anecdotes.question: What was my favorite flavor of baby food?

anecdotes.story: Most Asians have a purple birthmark covering their back and legs for the first couple years of their life. I don't really know why it's called a Mongolian spot, but the mongols were pretty fearsome warriors. Like me.
anecdotes.question: What kind of birthmark did I have on my back?

anecdotes.story: I loved jumping and bouncing and exercising my legs. Even before I had a bouncer chair, I always found ways to bounce. And then Mom got me my super bouncer. It. Was. Awesome!
anecdotes.question: What was my favorite activity as a baby?

anecdotes.story: Movies that have more action than story, or just too much action, are not exciting to me. I also confess to not appreciating the Lord of the Rings movies. Sorry.
anecdotes.question: Which of these types of movies do I NOT enjoy?
answers: Mormon” movies, for example Mobsters and Mormons, The RM, etc.

anecdotes.story: My siblings and parents know this, so it’s no surprise to you that I was born in Utah. While living in England, we were playing a getting to know you game at a RS activity, and we had to write something about ourselves we thought people might not know. I wrote that I was born in Utah. When that came up, everyone was rather surprised that I was originally from Utah. Kind of funny.
anecdotes.question: Where was I born?

anecdotes.story: Buddy, Hurley, Shadow, and Lightweight were our dogs. Shea, Thomas, Banana, Moose, Zeus, Poseidon, Inko, and Kawaii were our parakeets. We’ve also had two gerbils (Mr. Squeaker and Gerbie) and some goldfish.
anecdotes.question: Which of these isn’t the names of some of our pets?

anecdotes.story: If a voice counted as a musical instrument the answer would be six.
anecdotes.question: How many instruments can I play well?
answers: None - I really don't like music

anecdotes.story: For some reason frosting makes me gag.
anecdotes.question: What birthday food will I not eat?

anecdotes.story: The London Eye is a huge ferris wheel in the middle of London. It is 443 feet tall and takes about 30 minutes to complete one rotation. You get to look down on London, and the view is amazing.
anecdotes.question: What is the best birthday present I have ever received?
answers: A ride on the London Eye

anecdotes.story: Computer programming is awesome. My grandpa does it, and also you can get paid quite a lot.
anecdotes.question: When I grow up I want to be:

anecdotes.story: A baby deer was in the church parking lot and kids started chasing it so it ran out and tackled one of the kids then it ran off down the road.
anecdotes.question: My craziest encounter with an animal was with:

anecdotes.story: We’ve had lots of dogs in my lifetime. My favorite one was Shadow, one of the cute puppies we rescued from the WalMart parking lot on our way to the Airshow at the AF Base in Little Rock, Arkansas. She was sooooo cute! Her brother Lightweight was also adorable. Sadly, we didn’t have the means to take care of their health issues, and Mom had to take them to the pound. She cried all the way home.
anecdotes.question: What’s my favorite pet?

anecdotes.story: I have 4 black underarmor shirts and one yellow one. I have 6 zip-up hoodies: GAP, Disneyland Paris, Greece, a plain gray one, and Italia.
anecdotes.question: A necessity in my daily wardrobe is:
answers: Underarmor athletic shirt and at least one hoodie

anecdotes.story: One of my favorite things to do is to play spies, and I use it for getting Daniel to brush his teeth.
anecdotes.question: How do I help Daniel brush his teeth?

anecdotes.story: I like science because I get to do experiments. My favorite experiment was when we got to measure weights using scales.
anecdotes.question: What is my favorite subject at school?

anecdotes.story: In Minecraft I get to build stuff and survive. I play it with Justin.
anecdotes.question: What’s my favorite thing to do?

anecdotes.story: I was having the time of my life on the trampoline, flying up and down and over and down  – onto one of the springs at the edge. Ouch! That hurt!! It appeared that a wedge had been cut out of my lower lip. Mom searched the ground for the apparently missing chunk of lip. It turned out to be just a cut. We hopped in the van and drove to the base ER. By this time, I was done crying and the bleeding had mostly stopped. I was playing with the two sides of my lip. I thought it was pretty neat. Mom thought it was pretty gross. It was determined that I’d have to come back at around 3 in the morning because I had just eaten dinner and couldn’t get anesthetized for a few more hours. So we went home. At 2:30-ish a.m., Mom put me in the van and we went back for the stitches.
anecdotes.question: What was my mom looking for under the trampoline one evening?
answers: A chunk of my lower lip.

anecdotes.story: Dr. Seuss’ stories are so funny and fun to listen to and read. I like his funny words and rhyming.
anecdotes.question: Who is my favorite author?

anecdotes.story: I really loved our Edelweiss trip because that’s where I got my green emerald, and because we got to go swimming every day, and we stayed in an epic cabin with a TV and microwave (in which we made popcorn) and mini fridge. The bathroom and laundry room were really close to our cabin.
anecdotes.question: What was my favorite vacation?

anecdotes.story: As so often happens when the kids are supposed to be getting ready for bed, they play around. This particular night, the game was Jumping on the Bed. Need I say more? I was bounced a little too high and flew over to the radiator, my forehead hitting its top edge. I was sliced open and I got a trip to the ER, where I was wrapped tightly in a blanket so I couldn’t move too much, given numbing shots, and was stitched up. During each stitch, I’d hold tensely still. After each one, I’d relax and ask my mom if they were done. I was so brave.
anecdotes.question: Why did I go to the hospital instead of going to bed one night?
answers: I got to see a new baby with my Mom.
I got something stuck somewhere it shouldn’t have been.
My mom had to take one of my brothers.
I got hurt falling off the bed

anecdotes.story: Rob is the only guy I've ever kissed.
anecdotes.question: Who was the first guy that kissed me?

anecdotes.story: Becoming a mother. I was completely blown away by how much I loved that little baby Tanner, and each kid thereafter.
anecdotes.question: What was the biggest surprise of my life?
answers: When Mom and Dad bought me a pony
Ernie and Bert aren't real people

anecdotes.story: I was running downhill as fast as I could and I ignored Emily's shouts to stop because I thought she just wanted to beat me. Then I smacked right into a bunch of 2x4s sticking out of the back of a pickup truck.
anecdotes.question: What stopped me when I was running on the sidewalk?

anecdotes.story: In Hawaii I was bit by a centipede and my arm swelled up like a baseball and changed color with my pulse where I was bit.
anecdotes.question: What made it so I could see my pulse?

anecdotes.story: When I was 3 or 4 I went out into my back yard and stood on an ant hill in my underwear, I think my parents were working in the garden, and then my mother saw me with ants crawling up and down my legs, screamed, carried me into the kitchen and washed off all the ants on me in the sink.
anecdotes.question: When I was about three years old I was attacked by:

anecdotes.story: When I was in grad school I went to the doctor because I had a sore stomach and thought it was an ulcer and the only problem they could find with me was that I was hungry.
anecdotes.question: In grad school I went to the doctor once and he said I was:

anecdotes.story: When I was 5 I learned how you could take a cup of water and a straw and take water out by plugging the top of the straw and I could put it on a little mountain of dirt and make a mud clod which I could throw at the side of my house with a lovely little poof. I got in trouble.
anecdotes.question: I got in trouble once for decorating the house with:

anecdotes.story: When I was about 5 I found a lizard in my back yard and I chased it under a big round rock while my dad mowed the lawn. I started pushing the rock to roll it away so I could get at the lizard but it was too heavy and it fell back. So I pushed the other side and and kept pushing all around until my dad reached the rock with the lawn mower. He quickly pushed the rock out of the way and we both looked down to where the rock had been and there was a lizard completely smashed except its tail was separated from its body and was wiggling like crazy.
anecdotes.question: What was under the rock while I was helping my Dad mow the lawn?

anecdotes.story: When I was six I looked out the window in my bedroom and saw a trash truck drive up and a big tough man without a shirt hopped out of the truck with flies buzzing around him, picked up a trash can, and dumped its contents into the back of his truck, and I thought to myself, Wow, that's what I wanna be when I grow up.
anecdotes.question: What did I want to be when I grew up?

anecdotes.story: Kathryn, because she's great!
anecdotes.question: Who is my favorite superhero?

anecdotes.story: A rooster I was looking at hopped on my shoulder and pecked me on the cheek. (Not a kiss!) So they chopped its head off. And I had to get stitches.
anecdotes.question: When I was two years old, what happened to me at my dad’s boss’s house?
answers: I ate a hamburger that made me throw up seven times.
I got sprayed by a skunk.
I got bit by a rooster.

anecdotes.story: I was the Underhead. I used to put underwear on my head and socks on my hands and I was a superhero who could turn into different Underheads. It was awesome.
anecdotes.question: What name did I make up for myself when I was little?

anecdotes.story: I have always felt that paper is cold. Even in the desert.
anecdotes.question: I hate touching paper because…

anecdotes.story: Mom said if I didn’t clip my long nails, she’d paint them like a pretty lady. I wouldn’t clip them, so she painted them purple. I clipped them before school though, so I didn’t have to look like a pretty lady at school.
anecdotes.question: What did Mom do when I wouldn’t clip my fingernails?
answers: She took me to the police station.
She took me to a beauty parlor.

anecdotes.story: I was playing in the back yard and my friend hit the slide with a baseball bat. There was a wasp nest under the slide. The entire hive came out at me and I didn’t run. Ouch.
anecdotes.question: Where did wasps sting me when I was little?

anecdotes.story: I want to be a Mom, because my children will love me.
anecdotes.question: What do I want to be when I grow up?

anecdotes.story: I'm afraid to be alone downstairs because I think there are wolves that walk on two feet and hold sticks with spikes on the top, running slowly to kill me.
anecdotes.question: Why am I afraid to be alone downstairs?

anecdotes.story: I wanted jelly heels so bad.  Clear, plastic sandals with big, chunky heels.  Little flecks of glitter embedded in the plastic.  They even came in colors like pink, blue, and green.  But to my everlasting sorrow, Mom wouldn’t buy me a pair!
anecdotes.question: What piece of clothing did I want but never get?

anecdotes.story: In high school marching band, we played at all the home football games, and man, did it get cold at some of those games!  The metal in my mellophone would shrink in the cold air, and I’d try to keep my mouthpiece in my hand so it wouldn’t freeze to my lips when it was time to play.  One night, it decided to be stubborn and wouldn’t come out of the horn.  So, I grabbed it with my teeth and, sure enough, chipped one the peaks on a molar.  Sad day.
anecdotes.question: How did I chip one of my molars in high school?
answers: pulling a mouthpiece out of a mellophone

anecdotes.story: In marching band, you can never be sure which instrument is gonna smack you in the back of the head when you’re marching backwards. Well, for me, it was a trombone.  Which makes sense, since trombones have such long slides.  Fortunately, it only hit the ridiculous plume on my hat, knocking my hat askew.
anecdotes.question: Why was I reluctant to march backwards in marching band?
answers: I tripped and fell over once. Very embarrassing.
I stepped in dog poop. Gross.

anecdotes.story: Mom and Dad took me to New York City for my 18th birthday.  Outside of Macy’s department store, they still had the big logo for the Thanksgiving Day Parade painted on the street.  Mom told me to dance in the street, to which I replied, “No, Mom, I am not going to dance in the street!”  But I did hop down off the sidewalk and skip on the street.  So, close enough.
anecdotes.question: What did I do in New York that was almost cool?
answers: dance in the street where the Radio City Rockettes dance during the Thanksgiving Day Parade
eat a cake covered in gold leaf

anecdotes.story: Every time Grandpa Newberger sent me a letter or card, or talked to me on the phone, he told me to eat a tuna sandwich with pickles on it.  I, being sane, never did.
anecdotes.question: What have I never put on a sandwich?

anecdotes.story: Sometimes I go into super cleaning mode.  I like having a clean house, and sometimes that just takes too long.  So I’ll focus on a room and run around, putting away things that need putting away, sweeping, and wiping off counters.  Messes, fear the Tornado that leaves cleanliness in its wake!
anecdotes.question: Why does Mom call me “The White Tornado?”
answers: My superpower is to manipulate air.
I run around a room and clean with lightning speed.
I like to wear white dresses and spin around.
I have a white horse named tornado.

anecdotes.story: One morning in Japan, Kathryn and I were home alone. I opened the sliding door to let Barnaby out. Then it happened. The deadly geji-geji (a horrible, horrible centipede thing with a segmented body, and a bijillion long legs) fell into the dining room, and then skittered into the house. I screamed for dear life, slammed the door (leaving poor Barnaby in the back yard), and ran up to get Kathryn. She rolled over in bed and said, “just kill it yourself.” Completely abandoned, I went back to face the beast alone. I found some Raid and Windex. Terrified, I looked for the geji-geji. I took a few shaky steps into the room, armed with my poisonous household chemicals. Unable to see the beast, I turned slowly. To my everlasting horror, I saw the monster on the wall right where I had just been standing! I unleashed the entire contents of the raid and windex on the geji-geji. It tried to run, but I was triumphant! Mom and Dad came home to find a geji-geji drowned in a puddle of very clean-smelling liquid.
anecdotes.question: Why did Kathryn abandon me to the poisonous bug?
answers: She was just waking up and still groggy.
She was too cowardly to help me kill the bug.

anecdotes.story: A big Chocolate Chip cookie, believe it or not I told my mom that I didn’t really care about having a cake for my birthday that year so I just told her to make chocolate chip cookies instead.  What happened as a result is my mom just made one giant pizza sized chocolate chip cookie.
anecdotes.question: What kind of Birthday cake did I get on my 16th birthday?

anecdotes.story: When I was growing up I had a loose tooth.  So one day I was eating one of those little Debbie apple pies and suddenly I felt a crunch, just thinking it was an apple seed I decided to just keep chewing, eventually it broke and I swallowed it!  When the pie was gone I also noticed that I was missing my tooth.  Luckily the tooth fairy still came even though the tooth was going through my digestive system at the time.
anecdotes.question: What obscure item did I swallow once when I was growing up?

anecdotes.story: During one of my high school games there was a high ball that I jumped up to grab and there were two other players that were running to get the ball and I didn’t jump high enough to get over them however I did get the ball.  But as they tried to run underneath me one of them clipped my feet and I fell backwards right onto the back of my head.  It wasn’t a serious concussion I was still conscious and everything but it was still by definition a concussion.
anecdotes.question: What sport was I playing when I got my one and only concussion?

anecdotes.story: Mommy cuts my hair.  I sit on Daddy’s lap while he holds my head so I can’t wiggle around.  It’s pretty awful, but they let me watch Netflix on Mommy’s tablet while Mommy cuts, so I guess it’s ok.
anecdotes.question: Who cuts my hair?

anecdotes.story: Cars!  One time I even went downstairs by myself, got Cars, and brought it back up to the DVD player in the living room.  Mommy and Daddy are teaching me that I’m not allowed to touch Grandma and Grandpa’s movies, though.  Boo.
anecdotes.question: What is my favorite movie?

anecdotes.story: I stared at that snow the first time I saw it falling from the sky.  Mommy dressed me up in the white bear onesie/coat from Auntie Emily and walked me across the street to the library.  Lots of people told me I was so cute.  Thanks Auntie Emily!
anecdotes.question: What did I do the first time I saw snow?
answers: stared at it, cause I was pretty little

anecdotes.story: bean
anecdotes.question: Move along now.
